originalTitle: Iran war: What is happening on day 13 of US-Israel attacks?
title: News from Far Away: Big Changes for Everyone!
content:
Hello, Super Kids!

Hello, amazing young reporters from Africa! Today, we're going to talk about some news from far, far away. Sometimes, grown-ups in different countries don't always agree. When grown-ups don't agree and have big problems, it can cause changes for many people, all around the world.

Problems Far Away

Right now, there are some big disagreements happening between grown-ups in certain places very far from us. These problems can make things a little tricky for the whole world, even for us here in Africa. It's like when friends argue; sometimes, everyone around feels a little bit of the upset. We hope that people will find a way to agree and make peace soon, because being kind and helping each other is always the best way!

What are Cyberattacks?

Imagine you are playing your favourite game on a computer. What if someone tried to mess up your game from very far away? They might try to stop you from playing, or even change your game scores! That's a bit like what a cyberattack is. A cyberattack is when people use computers to try and cause problems for other computers. These other computers can belong to big companies, hospitals, or even governments. They try to get into other people's computer systems without being asked. Why is this a problem? If a big computer system for something important, like a bank or a power station, stops working because of a cyberattack, it can cause lots of trouble for everyone. People might not be able to get their money, or lights might go out! Did you know? Many countries have special teams of clever computer experts. These experts are like superheroes who guard their city from digital bad guys! They work hard to protect important computer systems from cyberattacks every single day.

The Narrow Water Road: Strait of Hormuz

Imagine a very, very busy road. But instead of cars, this road is full of giant ships! And instead of tarmac, it's made of water! There's a special, narrow water path far away called the Strait of Hormuz. It's like a tiny gate that big ships *must* go through. This strait is like a very important shortcut or a superhighway for ships. These ships carry all sorts of important things, especially something called 'oil', which we will talk about next. Many, many ships use this path every single day to travel between two very big seas. Why is this narrow water road so important? If there are problems or disagreements near this narrow water path, ships might find it hard or scary to go through. This can stop important things, like oil, from moving around the world. If ships can't pass easily, it causes big delays and problems. Did you know? The Strait of Hormuz is one of the busiest water roads in the entire world! Imagine thousands of giant ships using it every year, like a super-busy intersection for ocean travel!

What is Oil and Why is it So Important?

Have you ever ridden in a car, a bus, or seen a big airplane fly high in the sky? What makes them go? Fuel! A lot of that fuel comes from something very special called 'oil'. Oil is a black, thick liquid found deep, deep underground, hidden in the earth. We dig it up from the earth, clean it, and turn it into fuel for cars, buses, trucks, and planes. Oil is also used to make electricity for our homes, light our lamps, and even help factories make toys, clothes, and many other things we use every day. It's like the energy drink for our whole world! Why is it so special and important? Without oil, many of the things we use every day wouldn't work. Cars wouldn't drive, some lights wouldn't turn on, and it would be very hard to make and move all the things we need. It helps our world keep moving. Did you know? Dinosaurs lived millions and millions of years ago! Some of the oil we use today was formed from tiny plants and animals that lived a very, very long time ago, even before the dinosaurs walked the Earth!

Why Are Prices Going Up?

So, we have problems with grown-ups not agreeing in some countries. These problems can cause cyberattacks and make it tricky for ships to travel through that important water road, the Strait of Hormuz. And remember, many ships carry oil. When it's hard for ships to carry oil, or if people are worried about getting enough oil, there's less oil available for everyone in the world. When there's less of something important that everyone needs, its price usually goes up. It becomes more expensive to buy. What does this mean for us? If the price of oil goes up, it costs more to put fuel in buses and cars. This means it might cost a little more for shops to bring food, clothes, or other items to your town from faraway places. So, sometimes, things you buy might become a little more expensive. Did you know? When oil prices change, it can affect almost everything we buy! From the bread we eat for breakfast to the toys we play with, the cost of moving them around the world often depends on the price of oil!

How Does This Affect Us in Africa?

You might be thinking, "This is all happening very far away! How does it touch us here in Africa?" Well, let me tell you. Imagine dropping a little stone into a big puddle of water. Even if you drop the stone on one side, you'll see ripples go all the way to the other side of the puddle. That's a bit like how the world works! Problems happening far away can send small "ripples" or changes all the way to us. Specifically, when oil prices go up because of problems far away, it can make things a little more expensive here in our homes and shops. Our countries also need to buy oil to help us run our businesses, power our lights, and get around. If it costs more for them, it can cost a little more for us too. But remember, we are strong and smart! Our grown-ups work hard to make sure we have what we need. It's good to know what's happening in the world, even if it's far away. It helps us understand how everything is connected.

Let's Hope for Peace!

So, even though there are big problems far away with computers and ships carrying oil, we learn about them. We hope that the grown-ups involved will find a way to agree and make peace soon. Being kind, understanding, and helping each other is always the best way to solve problems and make the world a happier place for everyone!
summary: Big disagreements between countries far away are causing problems with computers, ships carrying oil, and making prices go up around the world, including here in Africa.
whyMatters: This matters because even problems far away can make small changes in our daily lives, like how much things cost in our shops, and it shows why everyone should try to get along.

originalTitle: Arsenal undone by own set-piece weapon as 100% record ends in Germany
title: Arsenal undone by own set-piece weapon as 100% record ends in Germany
content: "You do it too, eh?". Those were the words from Arsenal's set-piece coach Nicolas Jover to Bayer Leverkusen manager Kasper Hjulmand after the German side scored from a corner in the 1-1 Champions League last-16 first-leg draw between the two sides.
